title: 3D Reveal, Editorial Grid, @property, SVG Cursor, details Accordion
description: Five live UI demos: 3D perspective scroll reveal, CSS grid editorial layout, @property gradient text, context-aware SVG cursor, native details accordion.

drops.title: 3D Perspective Scroll Reveal
drops.description: Cards rotate from a tilted 3D plane to face-on as they enter the viewport — pure CSS 3D + IntersectionObserver.
drops.concept: Combining CSS perspective() transform with rotateX on an initial hidden state, then using IntersectionObserver to toggle a .revealed class, creates a satisfying 'landing' effect as cards enter the viewport. Staggered nth-child delays make the grid feel choreographed. The cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1) easing gives cards physical weight as they settle.

interaction.mouse: Hover cards for subtle border glow
interaction.scroll: Scroll to trigger each card's entrance
interaction.click: Sliders adjust tilt angle, duration, threshold

drops.title: Asymmetric Editorial Grid
drops.description: Magazine-style layout built entirely with CSS grid-template-areas — named zones that rearrange across 3 variants.
drops.concept: grid-template-areas turns complex editorial layouts into readable ASCII art. Declare zones by name (hero, lead1, side1…), place items with grid-area, and rearrange layouts by rewriting the areas string in a single media query. Three layout variants (Editorial, Magazine, Gallery) show how one DOM can serve completely different visual grids.
drops.source: CSS grid editorial layout patterns (CSS-Tricks / MDN)

interaction.click: Layout dropdown switches between 3 grid configurations

drops.title: @property Animated Gradient Text
drops.description: Five variants of CSS @property hue animation — per-word, per-character, sweep, wave, glow — showing why type registration unlocks gradient tweening.
drops.concept: Without @property, CSS custom properties are opaque strings — the browser can't interpolate hsl(var(--h),80%,60%) because it doesn't know --h is a number. Registering with syntax:'<number>' tells the browser to tween it, enabling smooth hue animation across gradients. Five variants demonstrate the range: staggered per-word, per-character shimmer, background-position sweep, translateY wave with color, and drop-shadow glow pulse.

interaction.mouse: Controls panel adjusts hue range, saturation, duration
interaction.scroll: Page scrollable to see all 5 variants

drops.title: Context-Aware SVG Morphing Cursor
drops.description: A custom cursor that changes shape based on hover context — circle, pointer ring, I-beam, crosshair, grab arrows, wait spinner.
drops.concept: Replacing the OS cursor with a custom SVG element gives full control over shape and animation. The CSS d property (animatable on paths with matching structure) allows smooth morphing between shapes when geometrically compatible. mouseover detection reads element type or explicit data-cursor attributes to switch states contextually. A trail of fading dots behind the cursor adds tactile depth.

interaction.mouse: Move cursor over different zones to see shape changes
interaction.click: Simulate Load button triggers spinner state

drops.title: Native details Accordion
drops.description: Zero-JavaScript accordion using ::details-content, details[name] exclusion, and interpolate-size: allow-keywords for real height:auto transitions.
drops.concept: Three brand-new CSS features finally make the accordion trivial: the name attribute on <details> for mutual-exclusion groups, ::details-content for targeting hidden content, and interpolate-size: allow-keywords to animate height:0→auto natively. The result is a fully keyboard-accessible, semantic accordion under 30 lines of CSS with no JavaScript, no ARIA attributes needed, and true progressive enhancement — browsers without interpolate-size still open/close, just without the animation.

interaction.click: Click any summary to open — others close automatically
interaction.keyboard: Tab to focus, Enter/Space to toggle
